semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw product data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format with cleaned column names and calculated fields. It prepares product information for downstream analytics by renaming columns, converting price units, and adding boolean flags to categorize products.

supporting_evidence.description: Column renaming (sku→product_id, name→product_name) and transformation (cents_to_dollars('price'))
Business rule implementation with coalesce functions to create is_food_item and is_drink_item flags
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_products and writes model.stg_products

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data by renaming fields, converting cent values to dollar amounts, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity for downstream analytics use.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming: id → order_id, store_id → location_id, customer → customer_id
Currency conversion from cents to dollars using cents_to_dollars macro
Timestamp truncation using dbt.date_trunc('day','ordered_at')

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw supply data from the ecom source into a standardized format for downstream analytics. It cleans and renames fields, generates a surrogate key, and converts cost from cents to dollars while preserving all original supply information.

supporting_evidence.description: dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['id', 'sku']) creates supply_uuid
cents_to_dollars('cost') as supply_cost converts pricing units
source reference to {{ source('ecom', 'raw_supplies') }}

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining staging tables for orders, products, and supplies to provide detailed information including product details, order timestamps, and aggregated supply costs for analytical reporting.

supporting_evidence.description: Joined query combining order_items, orders, products, and order_supplies_summary with key business fields
Aggregation of supply costs by product_id in order_supplies_summary CTE
Transformation dbt_model reads model.stg_order_items, model.stg_orders, model.stg_products and writes model.order_items

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive orders mart by enriching staged order data with summarized order item metrics and customer ordering sequence information. It provides key business insights including order cost breakdowns, food/drink item categorization, and customer order history ranking.

supporting_evidence.description: order_items_summary CTE computes supply costs, item counts and food/drink categorizations
compute_booleans CTE joins orders with item summaries and derives business flags like is_food_order
customer_order_count CTE uses window function to rank customer orders by sequence

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw store data into a standardized format by renaming fields and extracting date components. It serves as an intermediate layer between raw e-commerce store data and downstream analytics models.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming logic mapping raw store attributes to standardized column names (id→location_id, name→location_name)
Date truncation function applied to opened_at field to extract day-level granularity
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_stores and writes model.stg_locations

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw customer data from the ecom source by renaming columns to standardize field names, specifically mapping 'id' to 'customer_id' and 'name' to 'customer_name'. It serves as a standardized intermediate layer for downstream customer-related analytics and reporting.

supporting_evidence.description: Column renaming logic mapping 'id' to 'customer_id' and 'name' to 'customer_name'
Source reference to 'ecom' schema and 'raw_customers' table
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_customers and writes model.stg_customers

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Converts monetary values from cents to dollars format for consistent financial reporting across different database platforms.

supporting_evidence.description: Macro definition shows conversion from cents to dollars with database-specific implementations for numeric formatting
Transformation dbt_model reads none and writes model.cents_to_dollars

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce item data into a standardized format by renaming and selecting key fields such as order item ID, order ID, and product ID.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming logic mapping raw columns to business-friendly names: id → order_item_id, sku → product_id
Source reference to ecom.raw_items indicates input dataset for transformation
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_items and writes model.stg_order_items

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Organizes database schemas by routing seed data to raw schemas and managing custom schema naming conventions for production versus development environments.

supporting_evidence.description: Conditional logic for schema routing based on node.resource_type and target.name with explicit comments about seed data placement and production naming conventions
Transformation dbt_model reads none and writes model.generate_schema_name

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Creates a comprehensive customer analytics dataset that summarizes lifetime purchasing behavior, enabling customer segmentation and retention analysis.

supporting_evidence.description: Selects key customer metrics including lifetime orders, spend, and repeat purchase indicators for business analysis.
Calculates customer_type field to classify customers as new or returning for marketing segmentation.
Transformation dbt_model reads model.stg_customers, model.orders and writes model.customers

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Provides location data for business sites, supporting geographic analysis and location-based operations management.

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This file defines the data model, tests, semantic layer, metrics, and saved queries for order items in a business intelligence context, enabling revenue analysis and reporting by item, order, and time.

supporting_evidence.description: YAML structure containing models, unit_tests, semantic_models, metrics, and saved_queries sections defining business logic for order items
Semantic model definition with entities, dimensions, and measures including revenue calculations
Multiple metric definitions including simple, ratio, derived, and cumulative revenue metrics

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This file defines a data mart for order analytics, containing detailed order information including financial totals, item types, and customer associations. It supports business reporting through semantic models and predefined metrics for order volume, revenue, and customer behavior analysis.

supporting_evidence.description: models section defines the orders mart with description indicating key order details and granularity
unit_tests section contains test cases validating order item boolean conversions
semantic_models section defines order fact table with time dimension and entity relationships

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This file defines a customer data mart that aggregates key customer lifecycle metrics including lifetime spending, order counts, and customer type classification. It serves as a centralized source for customer analytics and supports downstream metric calculations like average order value.

supporting_evidence.description: YAML configuration defining customer mart with columns for lifetime spend, order counts, and customer type classification
Semantic model defining customer entities, dimensions, and measures for analytics
Metrics definitions including lifetime spend pretax and derived average order value calculation

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Establishes a standardized location dimension model with financial metrics for business intelligence, enabling analysis of location performance and tax implications.

supporting_evidence.description: Description states 'Location dimension table. The grain of the table is one row per location.' establishing standardized reporting structure.
Defines average_tax_rate measure which provides financial insights for business decision making.
